data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S6 1ba5: Docs slice)]

Audited this child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (S1 6d56, S2 70c7, S3 fa49, S4 30b3, S5 b7fa). This child is the DOCS slice (private-repo credential requirement, descriptive credential-failure behavior, ref/source + signing semantics, HMAC trust model + in-toto shape, REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR/disk-cap/EFS-flock caveat). The union of children covers every parent criterion. No uncovered parent criterion. Empty G3 findings list.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; this child's role called out):
- Parent AC1 (no attested gate reads the working tree; all read a faithful snapshot at the pinned SHA) — FULLY covered by S1 (6d56 materialize core) + S3 (fa49 gate re-rooting). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this docs child (its Scope: "Out: code (S1-S5)"); claims none. Not an absence (S1/S3 cover it).
- Parent AC2 (signed verdict records verified_at_sha + reproducible over identical content) — FULLY covered by S4 (30b3 record+sign+in-toto) with the immutable-SHA basis from S1, surfaced by S5. This child DOCUMENTS the signing semantics (its AC3: "ref/source semantics, origin/main default, AND the signs-vs-never-signs + verified_at_sha recording are documented for CLI and MCP") + the in-toto Statement shape (its AC4). Documentation of, not ownership of, the behavior. Not an absence.
- Parent AC3 (local mode reads in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict) — covered by S3 (in-place read) + S4 (signing bar), surfaced by S5. This child DOCUMENTS the attested-signs/local-never-signs distinction (its AC2 fail-closed-attested-vs-local-still-runs + its AC3 signs-vs-never-signs). Documentation half; behavior owned by S3/S4. Not an absence.
- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates, no git-lock contention / cross-op corruption; atomic population; delete-on-last-close) — FULLY covered by S1 (atomic population) + S2 (single-flight, delete-on-last-close). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child; claims none. Not an absence.
- Parent AC5 (snapshot cache + janitor reclaim under pressure without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY covered by S2 (70c7). This child DOCUMENTS the disk-cap behavior + REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR knob (its AC5), but the reclaim BEHAVIOR is S2's. Documentation of the knob, not the reclaim itself. Not an absence.
- Parent AC6 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces a descriptive, actionable credential error) — covered as a SPLIT: S1 (6d56) RAISES the descriptive error (behavior), THIS child DOCUMENTS it. This child is the PRIMARY OWNER of the documentation half: its AC1 (README MCP-setup section + docs/jira-sync-setup.md state the private-repo fetch-credential requirement + how to supply it, grep-able) + its AC2 (the descriptive missing-credential error message + remedy and the fail-closed-attested/local-still-runs behavior are documented in a named file). The "actionable" facet of parent AC6 — telling the operator HOW to supply credentials — is precisely this child's contribution. Clean split; no uncovered half.
- Parent AC7 (portable: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — covered as a SPLIT: S1 (6d56) IMPLEMENTS portability (no external tar / gettempdir-mkdtemp / flock+mkdir fallback), THIS child DOCUMENTS the portability knobs — REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR (no hardcoded /tmp) + the EFS/NFS flock caveat (xref alto-fruit-punch) — in its AC5. Implementation owned by S1; the operator-facing knob/caveat docs owned here. Clean split; no uncovered half.
- Parent AC8 (all six child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all six children (this child is story #6 of 6 in the roster) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot ... never the server's mutable checkout ...; signed verdicts reproducible and bound to verified_at_sha; the unsigned local mode preserves the single-developer verify-before-push workflow") decomposes onto AC1-AC3, all behaviorally owned by S1-S5; this docs child reinforces the operator/agent-facing comprehension of those guarantees (ref/source/signing semantics, origin/main default, HMAC trust-model limits) but introduces no behavioral obligation of its own and leaves no clause uncovered.

Consumer/delegation cross-checks (all covered-by-named-producer, not absences):
- The descriptive credential ERROR this child documents is RAISED by S1 (6d56 AC5: "private-repo fetch failure surfaces a descriptive, actionable credential error") — producer present; this child's AC1/AC2 document it. Covered split.
- The HMAC trust-model + in-toto Statement shape this child documents (its AC4) is BUILT by S4 (30b3: "in-toto Statement envelope"; S4's own "What" explicitly says its HMAC limits "are DOCUMENTED in S6") — the documentation hand-off is named by S4 and claimed here. Bidirectionally acknowledged; clean.
- The ref/source SEMANTICS + origin/main default this child documents (its AC3) are surfaced by S5 (b7fa, whose Scope explicitly delegates "prose docs (S6)") and threaded by S3 — producers present; prose claimed here. Clean.
- REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR / disk-cap (S2) / EFS-flock caveat (alto-fruit-punch, an out-of-scope tracker named in the parent) — the disk-cap behavior is S2's, the knob/caveat docs are this child's AC5; xref resolves to a real tracker. Clean.

Coverage-pairing note (clean for G3): this child and S5 both touch "documentation" — but S5 owns the machine-facing schema-text (outputSchema/defaults inline) while this child owns the human PROSE (named doc files: README MCP-setup, docs/jira-sync-setup.md, HMAC trust-model doc). Each AC here is pinned to a named, grep-able doc file, so the prose obligation is claimed exactly once (here). No double-ownership and no coverage gap. (Any WHO-writes-which-artifact seam is a G4 matter, not a G3 union-coverage gap.)

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the documentation slice of the parent criteria — it is the PRIMARY OWNER of the documentation half of parent AC6 (private-repo credential requirement + actionable remedy, grep-able) and AC7 (REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR / EFS-flock portability knobs), and documents the ref/source/signing semantics + HMAC trust model underpinning AC2/AC3/AC5. No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the roster. No G3 finding for this pairing.
